title: Discrete-time hazard with driver/race frailty
summary: This target is a one-step hazard.
rationale: This target is a one-step hazard. Discrete-time survival models are a natural fit, and frailty terms are specifically designed for hidden heterogeneity across clusters; here, driver/race/stint can induce unobserved pit propensity that plain boosted trees may only partially capture.
implementation_hint: Create person-period labels within each driver-race-stint for time to next pit. Fit a discrete-time hazard model with a flexible baseline over tyre age/lap and add grouped random-effect proxies or empirical-Bayes target statistics for driver-race/stint frailty. Convert the predicted hazard at horizon 1 back to PitNextLap probability, then blend with the current tree stack.
expected_effect: Can add low-correlation signal by modeling transition risk directly rather than post-hoc smoothing binary probabilities.
risk: Long-format construction and frailty estimation can overfit or become expensive; careful OOF generation is mandatory.
